---
title: "Generate a Locale from `/etc/locale.gen`"
subtitle: "Note#203"
id: "203"
date: 2026-03-30
date-modified: ""
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
On Debian-based systems, you can enable a locale in `/etc/locale.gen` and generate it directly, without using `dpkg-reconfigure locales`.

## Procedure

Enable the locale you want in `/etc/locale.gen`:

```bash
sed -i 's/^# *en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8$/en_US.UTF-8 UTF-8/' /etc/locale.gen
```

Or edit the file manually:

```bash
vim /etc/locale.gen
```

Generate the enabled locales:

```bash
locale-gen
```

Set the system default locale if needed:

```bash
update-locale LANG=en_US.UTF-8
```

## Verify

Check that the locale was generated:

```bash
locale -a | grep en_US
```

Check the current default locale:

```bash
locale
```
